checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=900000000 (2022-12-31) -> 1227000000 (2024-12-31), a 36% increase, while RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax=4796000000 (2022 annual) -> 4550000000 (2024 annual), a 5% decrease

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Assets=1813000000 (2024-06-30) -> 3392000000 (2024-09-30), nearly doubling in one quarter, while RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax grew only 930000000 -> 1040000000 (12% q/q) over the same period
evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet and income statement Q3 2024

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-290000000 (2024 annual) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-12000000 (2024 annual), a $278M gap
NetIncomeLoss=92000000 (2022 annual) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=310000000 (2022 annual), a $218M gap
NetIncomeLoss=-243000000 (2024-07-01 to 2024-09-30) coincides with the Coyote acquisition quarter (Goodwill and Intangibles jumped in same quarter)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet, CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold, or PurchasedTransportationExpense concepts were provided in the dataset for RXO (a non-asset-based freight brokerage, which typically does not report inventory)
evidence.location: Data set - absence of inventory/COGS tags across all filings provided

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss swung from -15000000 (Q1 2024) to -7000000 (Q2 2024) to -243000000 (Q3 2024)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=13000000 (2022-12-31) and =13000000 (2024-12-31) - essentially flat - while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=900000000 (2022-12-31) grew to 1227000000 (2024-12-31), a 36% increase, dropping the allowance/AR coverage ratio from ~1.44% to ~1.06%
evidence.location: 10-Q filed 2025-11-06, comparative allowance and AR balances

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: All 10-K and 10-Q filings appear on a regular annual/quarterly cadence (10-K filed 2023-02-24, 2024-02-13, 2025-02-27; 10-Q filings filed within ~35-95 days of period end) with no NT 10-K/NT 10-Q or 10-K/A, 10-Q/A restatement filings present in the chronology

Only minor Form 4/A insider-ownership amendments observed (4/A filed 2023-03-08, 2024-04-11, 2025-03-05, 2025-11-19); elevated 8-K frequency in Aug-Dec 2024 coincides with disclosed capital markets activity (424B5, S-3ASR, 424B7) tied to acquisition financing

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans FY2020 through Q3 2025 with both annual and quarterly granularity for key line items (Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingCashFlow, Assets, Receivables, Goodwill, Intangibles, Allowance)
Coverage gaps exist for inventory, cost of goods sold/purchased transportation expense, deferred revenue, and detailed reserve/contingency disclosures, limiting CL4 assessment
evidence.location: Absence of these concepts across the entire dataset

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase accounting for the Q3 2024 acquisition (evidenced by Goodwill jumping from 630000000 (2024-06-30) to 1027000000 (2024-09-30) and IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill jumping from 62000000 (2024-06-30) to 565000000 (2024-09-30)) appears to have driven a large non-cash charge (transaction costs, intangible amortization step-up, or fair-value remeasurement) that produced NetIncomeLoss=-243000000 (2024-07-01 to 2024-09-30) and full-year NetIncomeLoss=-290000000 (2024 annual), while NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities was only -12000000 (2024 annual). The magnitude and non-cash nature of these purchase-accounting adjustments create risk of misallocation between expense timing and capitalized intangible value, potentially understating current-period operating income or overstating intangible asset values assigned in the acquisition.

Receivables reserve (allowance for doubtful accounts) was not scaled up commensurate with a 36% increase in gross receivables following the 2024 acquisition (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=13000000 (2022-12-31) vs 13000000 (2024-12-31), while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=900000000 (2022-12-31) grew to 1227000000 (2024-12-31)). This could reflect under-provisioning for credit risk in the acquired receivables book, overstating net receivables and understating bad debt expense.

Consolidation of an acquired entity's receivables balance in a quarter where only a partial/stub period of its revenue is reflected (AR +57% q/q from 725000000 (2024-06-30) to 1138000000 (2024-09-30) vs revenue +12% q/q from 930000000 to 1040000000 for the same quarter) creates a mechanical AR/revenue mismatch. While largely attributable to acquisition-date consolidation, the scale of the mismatch warrants scrutiny of revenue cut-off and collection timing in the newly combined entity going forward.

top_signals: Goodwill and intangibles more than doubled in a single quarter (Q3 2024) coincident with a large net loss quarter (NetIncomeLoss=-243000000), consistent with acquisition-related non-cash charges/purchase accounting complexity
Persistent and widening divergence between NetIncomeLoss and operating cash flow, especially FY2024 (NetIncomeLoss=-290000000 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-12000000)
Allowance for doubtful accounts held flat at $13M despite a 36% increase in accounts receivable from 2022 to 2024, reducing reserve coverage ratio
Accounts receivable grew far faster than revenue in the acquisition quarter (Q3 2024), a mechanical consolidation effect that still merits monitoring for collectability and cut-off risk
No evidence of late filings, NT forms, or financial statement restatements (10-K/A, 10-Q/A) in the filing chronology, which is a mitigating factor
